rule: 0=no usable provenance; 1=weak/context family signal; 2=inventory-backed family signal; 3=exact paper-HF edge or exact/canonical model signal
interpretation: captures the strength of evidence for upstream/family curation

rule: 0=no explicit release; 1=stated/incomplete release; 2=code/scripts/recipe; 3=checkpoint/adapter/data/eval asset; 4=multiple assets or full recipe
interpretation: captures whether the paper record supports inspection, reuse, or preservation

rule: 0=no direct signal; 1=one of exact HF edge, paper HF link, or paper GitHub link; 2=two or more link signals
interpretation: captures paper-repository alignment signals; this is not a complete cross-platform graph
records jointly support usable provenance, concrete release assets, and at least one paper-repository link signal

rule: provenance>=2 and release>=2, but not high
interpretation: records support usable provenance and code/recipe-or-stronger release evidence, even if direct link evidence is incomplete

rule: at least one useful provenance, release, or link signal, but below operational threshold
interpretation: records are useful for some curation tasks but not a complete curation workflow

rule: no usable provenance, no explicit release, and no direct paper-repository link signal
interpretation: records expose too little evidence for reliable artifact curation
